purpose: Defines the data contract for a SimulationQuestion. A SimulationQuestion binds a hypothesis to the compared DeckVersions, the policy, the target metrics, and the success interpretation. It carries no metrics, results, or interpretation, and it does not itself execute a run.

includes: Required SimulationQuestion fields and their meaning.
The requirement that a question reference the policy and exact DeckVersions by identity.
The boundary that a question never carries results.
excludes: Metric values, raw counts, probabilities, or intervals.
Reasoning interpretation or Product Owner decisions.

policy_id.description: The SimulationPolicy policy_id this question runs under.

policy_version.description: The SimulationPolicy policy_version this question runs under.

hypothesis.description: The bounded hypothesis under test, phrased within the evidence-language boundary.

question_text.description: The explicit question, phrased about the simulated model, not gameplay.

compared_versions.description: Exactly the DeckVersions compared. Each entry records deck_version_id, path, run_role, and deck_content_fingerprint.

target_metrics.description: The metric_ids from the policy metric_catalog this question targets, each with its target_turn.

success_interpretation.description: How results would be read: the directional expectation and any threshold. This is interpretation guidance, not a recorded result.

execution_status.description: Task 30 questions are 'not_executed'. A question becomes 'executed' only when a real SimulationRun and SimulationResult exist in a later task.

limitations.description: Known limitations of the question scope.

boundary_rules: A SimulationQuestion references the policy by policy_id and policy_version; it never restates policy semantics.
A SimulationQuestion references DeckVersions by id, path, and deck_content_fingerprint; it never copies deck content.
A SimulationQuestion carries no metric values, raw counts, probabilities, or confidence intervals.
execution_status 'not_executed' forbids any accompanying SimulationRun or SimulationResult payload.
The hypothesis and question_text must obey the policy evidence_language_boundary and contain no forbidden gameplay/win-rate/quality claim.
validation_expectations: The question JSON parses and has every required field.
policy_id and policy_version match the SimulationPolicy.
Every compared_versions entry resolves to an existing DeckVersion whose recomputed deck_content_fingerprint equals the recorded value.
Every target_metrics metric_id exists in the policy metric_catalog and its target_turn does not exceed the observation horizon.
execution_status is 'not_executed' in Task 30 and no result fields are present.
explicit_boundary.carries_results is false, authorizes_deck_change is false, and is_gameplay_claim is false.
No forbidden evidence-language claim appears in the hypothesis or question_text.
The companion Markdown states the hypothesis, the compared versions, the target metrics, and the not-executed status.
